input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

- Download from [https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download)
- Pick the **latest LTS release** (currently .NET 8). The SDK includes the `dotnet` CLI.
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 2. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- Install from the **Mac App Store** (ensures signing/notarization).
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install          # command-line tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept # accept license
  ```
- Verify: `xcodebuild -version`

## 3. Install Android SDK (for Android)

**Option A — Android Studio (recommended):**
- Download from [https://developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- Use its SDK Manager to install: Android SDK Platform (API 34+), Build-Tools, Command-line Tools, and an emulator system image.

**Option B — CLI only:**
- Download command-line tools from the same page, then:
  ```
  sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" \
             "build-tools;34.0.0" "emulator" \
             "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
  ```
- Set `ANDROID_HOME` in your shell profile.
- Verify: `adb --version` and `emulator -list-avds`

## 4. Install .NET MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in the iOS, Android, and platform-specific build toolchains.
- Verify: `dotnet workload list` — you should see `maui` (and its sub-workloads like `maui-ios`, `maui-android`).

## 5. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [https://code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [https://www.jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider) |

## 6. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
dotnet workload search maui   # shows available MAUI workload versions
dotnet sdk check              # shows installed vs available SDK versions
xcodebuild -showsdks          # lists installed Apple SDK versions
sdkmanager --list              # lists installed/available Android packages
```

## 7. Verify Everything End-to-End

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for both platforms
dotnet build -f net8.0-android
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet run -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet run -f net8.0-ios
```

If both builds succeed and the app launches on a simulator/emulator, your environment is fully working.

## Key Gotcha Checklist

- **JDK**: The MAUI workload bundles a compatible JDK. If you hit Java errors, install **Microsoft OpenJDK 17** from [https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download) and set `JAVA_HOME`.
- **Xcode version**: Each .NET MAUI release supports a specific Xcode range — check the [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) if builds fail.
- **Apple Silicon**: All tools above are ARM-native. Use the `arm64-v8a` Android system image for emulator performance.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a comprehensive setup guide for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. This is a knowledge/guidance question — no code changes or repo exploration needed. Let me provide a thorough, practical answer.
